When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.

If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the palette nodes at row 57:
- Node 35: R at (57, 9)
- Node 36: p at (57, 16)
- Node 37: N at (57, 23)
- Node 38: O at (57, 30)
- Node 39: M at (57, 37)
- Node 40: b at (57, 44)
- Node 41: Y at (57, 51)
And the slots:
- Red container (row 22): slots at (22,22), (22,28), (22,40) - but (22,34) is 'c' (charcoal, not a slot)
- Green container (row 36): slots at (36,22), (36,28), (36,34), (36,40)
Wait, the red container has a 'c' (charcoal) at (22,34) which is not a slot. Let me look at the red container more carefully. The red container has:
- Slot at (22,22) - g (gray)
- Slot at (22,28) - g (gray)
- (22,34) - c (charcoal) - NOT a slot!
- Slot at (22,40) - g (gray)
So the red container has 3 slots: (22,22), (22,28), (22,40)
And the green container has 4 slots: (36,22), (36,28), (36,34), (36,40)
Now let me think about the palette order: R, p, N, O, M, b, Y
And the top row order: O, p, R, b, N, Y, M
These are DIFFERENT orders! The palette is NOT in the same order as the top row.
Palette: R, p, N, O, M, b, Y
Top row: O, p, R, b, N, Y, M
This is important! The palette order might be the intended order for filling slots.
Let me think about what the 159-frame animation configuration was again. I need to recall from history...
Actually, let me try a new hypothesis. What if the palette order IS the intended filling order? So:
- First 3 palette colors go to red container: R, p, N
- Last 4 palette colors go to green container: O, M, b, Y
Let me try this: Red: R, p, N | Green: O, M, b, Y
Wait, but I need to figure out the slot positions. The red container has 3 slots and the green has 4 slots.
Red slots (left to right): (22,22), (22,28), (22,40)
Green slots (left to right): (36,22), (36,28), (36,34), (36,40)
Palette colors (left to right): R(9), p(16), N(23), O(30), M(37), b(44), Y(51)
Let me try filling in palette order:
1. Red slot 1 ← R
2. Red slot 2 ← p
3. Red slot 3 ← N
4. Green slot 1 ← O
5. Green slot 2 ← M
6. Green slot 3 ← b
7. Green slot 4 ← Y
Then SPACE.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# New hypothesis: palette order is the filling order
# Palette: R, p, N, O, M, b, Y (left to right)
# Red slots (3): R, p, N
# Green slots (4): O, M, b, Y

# Slot centers:
# Red: (22,22), (22,28), (22,40)
# Green: (36,22), (36,28), (36,34), (36,40)
# Palette centers: R(58,10), p(58,17), N(58,24), O(58,31), M(58,38), b(58,45), Y(58,52)

# Fill in palette order
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 10},   # R
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 22, 'col': 22},    # Red slot 1
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 17},   # p
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 22, 'col': 28},    # Red slot 2
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 24},   # N
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 22, 'col': 40},    # Red slot 3
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 31},   # O
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 22},    # Green slot 1
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 38},   # M
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 28},    # Green slot 2
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 45},   # b
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 34},    # Green slot 3
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 52},   # Y
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 40},    # Green slot 4
    {'action': 'SPACE'}
])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
